note: Equation index built from the FE engine's cumulative cheat sheet / FE_Question_Bank 'Equations & Concepts' (Days 1-4). Section names + Ctrl-F terms match the NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6. Grows as new study days are generated.

entries.note: Laminar Re<2100; turbulent Re>10^4
Turbulent f: read from the Moody chart
Colebrook — NOT in the FE Handbook; use the Moody chart
Handbook uses C (not K)

entries.eq: $\dot W_{fluid}=\gamma Q H,\quad \dot W_{brake}=\dfrac{\gamma Q H}{\eta}$

entries.note: Series: heads add; parallel: flows add — concept, not stated in Handbook
Fr<1 subcritical, =1 critical, >1 supercritical
In Handbook (p.296, capital I). C dimensionless, 0<=C<=1; 1 in/hr over 1 acre ~ 1 cfs
SUPPLEMENTAL — not in Handbook v10.6 (US form only); memorize
Area-weighted; standard practice
In Handbook (p.296, inches). Higher CN -> smaller S -> more runoff. SI $S=25400/CN-254$ mm is supplemental (not in Handbook)
In Handbook (p.296). Q=0 when P<=0.2S; Q,P,S in inches. $I_a=0.2S$ implied (not stated separately)
US: depth(in)/12 x area(ac)=ac-ft. SI: depth(m) x area(m^2)=m^3
SUPPLEMENTAL — not printed in Handbook v10.6. P = annual exceedance probability of the T-year event
SUPPLEMENTAL — not in Handbook v10.6. Probability of >=1 exceedance in n years

entries.section: Surface Water — Rainfall and Runoff (IDF, supplemental)
entries.note: SUPPLEMENTAL — not in Handbook v10.6. For fixed return period, intensity falls as duration rises
In Handbook (Geotechnical). Higher e and n → more storage space.
In Handbook p.297. Q = discharge; q = Darcy (superficial) velocity; v = seepage velocity; n = effective porosity; i = hydraulic gradient = dh/dx.
In Handbook p.297. Q = total volume collected; i = applied gradient; A = specimen area; te = elapsed time.
In Handbook p.297. a = standpipe area; L = specimen length; A = specimen area; h1, h2 = initial/final heads.
In Handbook p.297. b = saturated aquifer thickness. Units: L²/T (e.g. m²/s or ft²/s).
In Handbook p.298. Steady-state pumping from unconfined aquifer. h1,h2 = saturated heights at r1,r2. Specific capacity = Q/Dw.
In Handbook p.299. Steady-state pumping from confined aquifer. h1,h2 = piezometric head at r1,r2. T = Kb.

In Handbook (Environmental Eng.). Contaminant velocity = v/R; R=1 for conservative tracer. ρb = bulk density; ne = effective porosity; Koc = organic carbon partition coeff; foc = organic carbon fraction.
In Handbook (Environmental Eng. p.330). D = max depth; Rv = retention constant (soil+product specific, tabulated); V = spill volume; A = spill area.
Handbook p.348 Common Radicals table: EW(Ca2+)=20.0, EW(Mg2+)=12.2, EW(CaCO3)=50.0, EW(HCO3-)=61.0. Total hardness = Ca hardness + Mg hardness.
In Handbook p.339. D1,D2 = initial/final DO of diluted sample; P = fraction of wastewater sample; B1,B2 = seed control DO; f = fraction of seeded dilution water.
In Handbook p.327. Unit factor 8.34 lb·L/(mg·MG). SI equivalent: M (kg/d) = C (mg/L) × Q (m3/d) / 1000.
In Handbook p.327. Lo = ultimate BOD (mg/L); k = BOD decay rate constant base e (day-1); BODt = BOD exerted at time t. Remaining BOD = Lo·e^{-kt}.
In Handbook p.328. θ values: BOD (4–20°C)=1.135; BOD (21–30°C)=1.056; Reaeration=1.024; Bio Towers=1.035; Trickling Filters=1.072.
In Handbook p.331 reactor table. For first-order decay; θ = V/Q (HRT). CMFR is always less efficient than PFR for positive-order reactions.
In Handbook p.344. Primary clarifier: OFR avg 800–1200 gpd/ft2 (32–49 m/d); HRT 2 hr; depth 10–12 ft. vo = critical settling velocity for 100% removal of smallest particle.
In Handbook p.346. Applies in laminar settling (Re ≤ 1). ρp = particle density; ρf = fluid density; d = particle diameter; μ = dynamic viscosity. Also written using SG: vt = g·ρf·(SG-1)·d²/(18μ).
In Handbook p.351–353. C = residual Cl2 (mg/L); t10 = time for 10% of flow through reactor (min); BF = baffling factor (0.1 unbaffled → 1.0 plug flow). CT tables: Giardia 3-log, viruses 4-log.

In Handbook p.347. 7 reactions: (1) CO2 removal; (2) Ca-carbonate hardness; (3) Ca-non-carbonate; (4) Mg-carbonate; (5) Mg-non-carbonate; (6) excess alkalinity; (7) recarbonation. Soda ash Na2CO3 removes non-carbonate hardness.

In Handbook p.347 (reaction 1). Products: Al(OH)3 gelatinous floc (settles with enmeshed particles), CaSO4 (stays in solution), CO2. Effective at pH 6–8. FeCl3 and Fe2(SO4)3 are alternative coagulants.
In Handbook p.339. θc = SRT (sludge age, days); θ = HRT (days); Y = yield (kg/kg); S0-Se = BOD removed (mg/L); kd = endogenous decay rate (day-1). Typical MLSS 1500–3000 mg/L (conventional).
In Handbook p.339. SVI < 80 excellent; 80–150 acceptable; > 150 bulking sludge (poor settling). Units: mL/g.

In Handbook p.340. k = treatability constant (min-1, ~0.06 min-1 for municipal/modular plastic at 20°C); D = media depth (m); q = hydraulic loading [m3/(m2·min)]; n = media coefficient (n=0.5 modular plastic). kT = k20·(1.035)^(T-20).

In Handbook p.338. Ws = dry solids mass; s = solids content (%); γ = unit weight of water (62.4 lb/ft3 or 1000 kg/m3); S = specific gravity of wet sludge (~1.02). Converts dry-weight production to wet volume.
In Handbook p.341 (aerobic digestion design-criteria table). VSS reduction 40–50%. O2 requirement ~2.3 lb O2/lb cell tissue destroyed.
Fundamental stoichiometry — not a Handbook formula. ThOD sets the upper bound; COD ≤ ThOD. Example: glucose C6H12O6 (MW=180) + 6O2 → 6CO2+6H2O gives ThOD ratio = 192/180 = 1.067 g O2/g glucose.
Fundamental concept. Ratio ≥ 0.5: readily biodegradable; 0.3–0.5: moderately; < 0.3: poorly biodegradable / inhibitory. COD ≥ BOD5 always.

In Handbook p.339 (secondary clarifier mass balance). Full form: (Q0+QR)XA = QeXe + QRXr + QwXw. When effluent solids ≈ 0 and Qw << QR: QR = Q0 XA/(Xr-XA). Recycle ratio R = QR/Q0 typically 0.25–0.5 for conventional AS.
In Handbook p.339 (SRT definition rearranged). θc = V·XA / (QwXw + QeXe). Neglecting effluent solids: Qw = V·XA / (θc·Xw). Units must be consistent (m3/d and m3, or MG/d and MG).

In Handbook p.340 (Environmental Engineering, wastewater section). Cm = methanol (mg/L); No = nitrate-N (mg/L); Nl = nitrite-N (mg/L); Do = dissolved O2 (mg/L). The 0.87·Do term accounts for methanol consumed to deplete DO before denitrification proceeds.

In Handbook p.337 (NRC equation for single-stage rock trickling filter). R = recycle ratio QR/Q0. F appears in the NRC efficiency equation E1 = 100/[1+0.4432√(W/(VF))]. Higher R → higher F → improved effective capacity.

entries.eq: $v_o = Q/A_{surface}$; design range: 400\text{--}700\ \mathrm{gpd/ft^2}\ (16\text{--}28\ \mathrm{m/d})$ for air-AS (not ext. aeration)
entries.note: In Handbook p.345 (design criteria table). Following air-activated sludge (not extended aeration): avg 400–700 gpd/ft2 (16–28 m/d), peak 1000–1200 gpd/ft2 (40–64 m/d). Extended aeration secondary: avg 200–400 gpd/ft2 (8–16 m/d), lower OFR reflects longer SRT and higher MLSS.

In Handbook p.340. Solving for Se: Se = S0·E/(1+R−R·E) where E = exp(−kD/qT^n). R = QR/Q (recycle ratio); q = Q/A (base hydraulic loading without recycle); qT = (1+R)q (total loading with recycle). Recycle dilutes influent BOD but reduces per-pass efficiency; net effect on Se depends on parameters.

In Handbook p.342 (anaerobic digestion design criteria table). VS reduction: std 35–50%, hi-rate 45–55%. Gas ≈ 65% CH4, 35% CO2. Standard-rate = unheated/unmixed (stratified); high-rate = heated (35°C) + completely mixed.

In Handbook p.341 (aerobic digestion design criteria). O2 for cell tissue destruction = ~2.3 lb O2/lb VSS destroyed; for BOD5 in primary sludge = 1.6–1.9 lb O2/lb. VSS reduction 40–50%. Tank volume formula: V = Qi(Xi + F·Si)/[Xd(kd·Pv + 1/θc)].
Fundamental — NOT printed in Handbook v10.6. Strong acid: [H+] = Cacid; strong base: [OH-] = Cbase, then pH = 14−pOH. Kw = [H+][OH-] = 1.0×10-14 at 25°C.
SUPPLEMENTAL — NOT in Handbook v10.6. Always provided in stem on exam if needed. Buffer is most effective within ±1 unit of pKa.
Fundamental (EW table in Handbook p.348). EW(CaCO3)=50 g/eq; EW(HCO3-)=61 g/eq. Equivalently: meq/L × 50 mg/meq = mg/L as CaCO3.
In Handbook (Chemistry section). CaCO3 Ksp = 3.3×10-9; solubility = √Ksp = 5.7×10-5 mol/L = 2.3 mg/L as Ca2+. Common-ion effect: adding one ion suppresses the other.
In Handbook p.331 (Reactor Design table). Zero-order: rate = k0 (constant, mg/L·time); half-life depends on C0. Contrast first-order: t1/2 = 0.693/k (independent of C0).

entries.eq: $DoU=\dfrac{2C+2-H+N}{2}\quad(\text{O, S not counted; each halogen replaces 1 H})$
entries.note: Fundamental — NOT in Handbook v10.6. DoU=0: saturated alkane; DoU=1: one ring or C=C; DoU=4: benzene ring (3 C=C + 1 ring). Useful for identifying compound class from MW data.
Fundamental. Check DoU: alkane DoU=0; alkene/cyclo DoU=1; alkyne or diene DoU=2. Environmental significance: BTEX (aromatics, DoU=4) are priority groundwater pollutants.
In Handbook (Environmental Engineering section). Hcc > 0.01: significant volatilization; Hcc > 0.1: readily stripped. Air-stripping design exploits high Hcc to remove VOCs from groundwater.

In Handbook (Environmental Engineering section). High Kow → lipophilic, sorbs to soil organic carbon, bioaccumulates. log Kow > 5: PBT (persistent, bioaccumulative, toxic) concern. Low Kow → stays in water, more mobile.
In Handbook (Environmental Engineering p.328–329). BCF ≥ 1,000 L/kg: bioaccumulative (EPA Tier 2); BCF ≥ 5,000 L/kg: highly bioaccumulative (PBT status). Assumes tissue density ≈ 1 kg/L.

In Handbook p.330 (Environmental Engineering). Koc = organic carbon partition coefficient (mL/g); foc = fraction organic carbon; ρb = bulk density; ne = effective porosity. Koc empirically ≈ 0.41·Kow (supplemental — not in Handbook). Retardation entry also in Groundwater section.
Handbook p.334. Also called Algebraic Projection. k = growth rate (persons/yr); Δt = elapsed years. Assumes constant annual increment.
Handbook p.334. Also called Log Growth = Exponential Growth = Geometric Growth. k units: yr⁻¹. Compounding continuously.
Handbook p.334. k = annual growth fraction; n = number of periods. Discrete annual compounding.
Handbook p.334. Scales city population in proportion to its larger region.
Handbook p.334. S = saturation population. NOT called logistic in Handbook.
Handbook p.330–331 table. r = −kC. Effluent: Ct = C0/(1+kθ).
Handbook p.330–331 table. r = −kC. Effluent: Ct = C0·exp(−kθ). PFR more efficient than CMFR for first-order.
Handbook p.330–331 table. r = −k. Same HRT for all ideal reactor types.
Handbook p.330–331 table. r = −kC².
Handbook p.121. Engineering stress uses original area A0; true stress uses actual area A.
Handbook p.121. E = elastic modulus = modulus of elasticity = Young's modulus. Elastic range only.
Handbook p.127. Free thermal strain. If constrained: thermal stress σ = EαΔT.
Handbook p.131. δt = deformation; α = coefficient of expansion; L = length; T, T0 = final/initial temp.

entries.section: Civil Engineering — Fire Hydrant Rated Capacity
entries.term: Fire Hydrant Rated Capacity at 20 psi (NFPA 291)
entries.note: Handbook p.304. Source: NFPA Standard 291. QR, QF in gpm; pressures in psi.
Handbook p.304–305. Q = gpm; D = outlet diameter (in.); P = pitot pressure (psi); Cd = 0.90/0.80/0.70 per outlet geometry.
Handbook p.317. P = pressure (atm); MW = molecular weight (g/mol); R = 82.06 cm³·atm/(mol·K); T = temperature (K). At 25°C, 1 atm: C(µg/m³) = C(ppb)×MW/24.45.

Handbook p.318. Valid at y=0, z=0 (centerline at ground). Q = emission rate (g/s); σy, σz = horizontal/vertical dispersion (m); u = wind speed (m/s); H = effective stack height (m). Terrain reflection term omitted.
Derived from Gaussian formula. e ≈ 2.718. σy evaluated at the downwind distance where σz = H/√2. Not printed separately in Handbook v10.6 — derive from p.318 formula (supplemental).
Handbook p.319. Γ = actual (ambient) lapse rate; ΓAD = dry adiabatic lapse rate = 0.98°C/100 m. Pasquill-Gifford stability classes A–F also on p.319.
Handbook p.322. H = inlet height (ft or m); Lb = length of body; Lc = length of cone. Ne is dimensionless.
Handbook p.323. µ = gas viscosity; H = inlet height; W = inlet width; Ne = effective turns; Vi = inlet velocity; ρp−ρg = particle–gas density difference. Factor is 2, not π.
Handbook p.322. dpc = 50% cut diameter; dp = particle diameter. η → 1 for dp >> dpc.

entries.eq: $A/C = Q / A_{fabric}$, typical range: 1–3 m³/(min·m²) for pulse-jet/felt
entries.note: Handbook p.324. Q = volumetric gas flow; Afabric = total filter cloth area. Design ranges by fabric/cleaning type in Handbook table.
Handbook p.325. W = drift (migration) velocity (m/s or ft/s); A = collection plate area (m² or ft²); Q = volumetric gas flow. η expressed as fraction.
Handbook p.325. Win, Wout = mass feed rates of principal organic hazardous constituent (POHC) in/out.
Handbook p.325. Concentrations in same units (ppm or vol%). CE = 100% means no incomplete combustion products.
Handbook p.325. t = residence time (min); L = kiln length (ft); D = diameter (ft); S = slope (in/ft); N = rotational speed (rpm). USCS units only.
Handbook pp.325–326. Ciss = steady-state indoor conc. (µg/m³); Co = outdoor conc.; S = source strength (µg/s); V = room volume (m³); k = removal rate constant (s⁻¹); QOA = outdoor air flow (m³/s).
Handbook p.326. QOA = outdoor air flow (cfm); n = number of occupants; Cindoors, COA = indoor/outdoor CO2 concentrations (ppm). USCS formula.
Handbook p.326. CRA = return air conc.; CSA = supply air conc.; COA = outdoor air conc. All in same units (ppm CO2 or µg/m³).
Handbook p.326. N = outdoor ACH (h⁻¹); Ci = initial indoor conc.; Ca = final indoor conc.; Co = outdoor conc.; h = elapsed time (h).

entries.section: Landfill — Clay Liner Leachate Breakthrough Time
entries.eq: $t = \dfrac{d^2\,\eta}{K(d+h)}$  (d=liner thickness ft, \eta=porosity, K=hydraulic conductivity ft/yr, h=hydraulic head ft) — Handbook p.333
$N_A = \dfrac{D\,\eta_{\text{gas}}\,(C_{\text{fill}}-C_{\text{atm}})}{L}$  (D: CH\textsubscript{4}=0.20 cm²/s, CO\textsubscript{2}=0.13 cm²/s) — Handbook p.333
$\Delta S_{LC} = P - R - ET - PER_{sw}$  (all in inches; PERsw=percolation into waste) — Handbook p.333

entries.section: Landfill — Effect of Overburden Pressure on Specific Weight
entries.eq: $SW_p = SW_i + \dfrac{p}{a+b\,p}$  (SW in lb/yd³, p in lb/in², a in yd³/in², b in yd³/lb; typical SW_i=1000, SW_p=1750–2150) — Handbook p.333
$VR(\%) = \dfrac{V_i - V_f}{V_i} \times 100$  (Vi=initial vol yd³, Vf=final vol yd³) — Handbook p.334
$N = N_0\,e^{-0.693\,t/\tau}$  (N=activity, \tau=half-life, t=time in same units as \tau) — Handbook p.335
$\text{Risk} = CDI \times CSF$  (CDI=chronic daily intake mg/(kg·day), CSF=cancer slope factor (mg/kg·day)⁻¹; acceptable range 10⁻⁶ to 10⁻⁴) — Handbook p.25
$HI = \dfrac{CDI}{RfD}$  (HI > 1.0 = adverse effect possible; RfD = reference dose mg/(kg·day)) — Handbook p.26
$RfD = \dfrac{NOAEL}{UF}$,  $SHD = RfD \times W$  (NOAEL=no-observable-adverse-effect-level mg/(kg·day), UF=uncertainty factor, W=body weight kg) — Handbook p.26
$CDI = \dfrac{(C_W)(IR)(EF)(ED)}{(BW)(AT)}$  (CW=mg/L, IR=L/day, EF=days/yr, ED=yr, BW=kg, AT=days; carcinogen AT=75 yr×365) — Handbook p.29
$CDI = \dfrac{(C_A)(IR)(ET)(EF)(ED)}{(BW)(AT)}$  (CA=mg/m³, IR=m³/hr, ET=hr/day; adult IR=0.98 m³/hr 6-hr day) — Handbook p.29–30
$CDI = \dfrac{(C_S)(IR)(CF)(FI)(EF)(ED)}{(BW)(AT)}$  (CS=mg/kg, IR=mg/day, CF=10⁻⁶ kg/mg, FI=fraction ingested; child IR >100 mg/day) — Handbook p.29–30
$D = 100\% \times \sum_i \dfrac{C_i}{T_i}$  (Ci=hr at SPL, Ti=permissible hr; 90 dBA→8 hr, 95→4, 100→2, 105→1, 110→0.5, 115→0.25; D>100%→abatement) — Handbook pp.34–35
$PV = mRT, \quad R_i = \bar{R}/M_i, \quad \bar{R} = 8{,}314\;\text{J/(kmol·K)}$  (P in Pa, V in m³, m in kg, T in K) — Handbook p.144
$\dfrac{P_1 V_1}{T_1} = \dfrac{P_2 V_2}{T_2}$  (T in K; for fixed mass of ideal gas) — Handbook p.144
$P_1 V_1 = P_2 V_2$  (constant T, ideal gas; Pv = constant) — Handbook p.148
$V_1/T_1 = V_2/T_2$  (constant P, ideal gas; T/v = constant; T in K) — Handbook p.148
$Q - W = \Delta U$  (Q > 0 into system, W > 0 out of system; no ΔKE/ΔPE) — Handbook p.147
$\eta_c = \dfrac{T_H - T_L}{T_H}$  (TH, TL in absolute units K or °R; maximum possible heat-engine efficiency) — Handbook p.149
$P_1 V_1^k = P_2 V_2^k$,  $V_2 = V_1(P_1/P_2)^{1/k}$,  $k = c_p/c_v$  (air k≈1.4, monatomic k≈1.67) — Handbook p.148
